rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ZBS LAW, LLP RE: 555 4TH STREET UNIT 607
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Surplus Funds From Proceeds
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2021 line 1. NOTICE OF HEARING RE: SURPLUS FUNDS FROM PROCEEDS Claims by Chase is GRANTED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Enforcing Settlement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF KATHERINE IKEDA'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER ENFORCING SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6 Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on August 12, 2021 defendant to file a supplemental declaration addressing only the payment of September and November 2019 share of the monthly loan payment (See Plaintiff's Reply Declaration 4:18-19). Motion will be otherwise denied. Moving party failed to establish that Defendant breached the stipulation, as drafted and executed on August 12, 2019. Both sides seek admission of parol evidence. If parol evidence is admitted as to the parts of the agreement that are arguably vague, then on the issues in dispute, there will be no meeting of the minds, e.g. "property-related costs" other than loan, taxes and insurance; the "past owed" amount as of August 12, 2019 to include almost $100,000 demanded by the bank in December 2019, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VY NGUYEN VS. ROBERT A. PIKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 12, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANTS JANET KRAHLING AND PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden is show that the release in the consolidated class action is applicable to the case at bar, including evidence and argument that the claims in this action (failure to disclose sinking/tilting in March 2016 in a sale of a single unit) are factually related to the claims asserted in any consolidate action, e.g. no reference to specific causes of action or claims for comparison. Reyan's Pasta Bella v. Visa USA (2006) 442 F. 3d 741. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. Triable issues of fact as to the knowledge and failure to disclose sinking/tilting. Objection to Sean Jeffries deposition transcript is sustained. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
